title: Kibatalia microphylla
intro: Kibatalia microphylla, also known as the Philippine forest tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Kibatalia microphylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kibatalia microphylla is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat fever, cough, and skin problems, as well as to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Kibatalia microphylla belongs to the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family encompasses over 5,000 species and is widely distributed across the globe. Kibatalia microphylla is also classified as a genus of flowering plants within the family Apocynaceae. The genus Kibatalia is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in the Philippines and other surrounding islands. The classification of Kibatalia microphylla further solidifies its place within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
